query: Can you direct me to research that explores methods for transforming multi-hop questions into single-hop sub-questions to leverage existing single-hop answer models?
criteria: multi-hop question decomposition into single-hop sub-questions
transforming multi-hop questions to single-hop for QA models
question decomposition methods for multi-hop reasoning

title.value: Do Multi-Hop Question Answering Systems Know How to Answer the Single-Hop Sub-Questions?

recommendation_reason.value: We adopt a neural decomposition model to generate sub-questions for a multi-hop complex question, followed by extracting the corresponding sub-answers.

supported_research_question.value: We adopt a neural decomposition model to generate sub-questions for a multi-hop complex question, followed by extracting the corresponding sub-answers.

method.value: We adopt a neural decomposition model to generate sub-questions for a multi-hop complex question, followed by extracting the corresponding sub-answers.

data_or_scenario.value: Rapid progress has been made in the field of question answering (QA), thanks to the release of many large-scale, highquality QA datasets.

main_conclusion.value: This indicates that these models manage to answer the multi-hop questions using some partial clues, instead of truly understanding the reasoning paths.

limitations.value: We show that multiple state-of-the-art multi-hop QA models fail to correctly answer a large portion of subquestions, although their corresponding multi-hop questions are correctly answered.

relevance_strength.value: We adopt a neural decomposition model to generate sub-questions for a multi-hop complex question

title.value: Understanding and Improving Zero-shot Multi-hop Reasoning in Generative Question Answering

recommendation_reason.value: Generative question answering (QA) models generate answers to questions either solely based on the parameters of the model (the closed-book setting) or additionally retrieving relevant evidence (the open-book setting).

supported_research_question.value: We perform several studies aimed at better understanding the multi-hop reasoning capabilities of generative QA models.

method.value: we decompose multi-hop questions into multiple corresponding single-hop questions

data_or_scenario.value: when trained only on single-hop questions, models generalize poorly to multi-hop questions

main_conclusion.value: multi-hop reasoning does not emerge naturally in generative QA models, but can be encouraged by advances in training or modeling techniques

limitations.value: Generative QA models can answer some relatively complex questions, but the mechanism through which they do so is still poorly understood.

relevance_strength.value: Generative question answering (QA) models generate answers to questions either solely based on the parameters of the model (the closed-book setting) or additionally retrieving relevant evidence (the open-book setting).

title.value: Compositional Questions Do Not Necessitate Multi-hop Reasoning

recommendation_reason.value: single-hop reasoning can solve much more of the dataset than previously thought

supported_research_question.value: Our analysis is centered on HOTPOTQA, where we show that single-hop reasoning can solve much more of the dataset than previously thought.

method.value: introduce a single-hop BERT-based RC model that achieves 67 F1

data_or_scenario.value: Our analysis is centered on HOTPOTQA

main_conclusion.value: even highly compositional questions can be answered with a single hop if they target specific entity types, or the facts needed to answer them are redundant.

limitations.value: We also design an evaluation setting where humans are not shown all of the necessary paragraphs for the intended multi-hop reasoning but can still answer over 80% of questions.

relevance_strength.value: single-hop reasoning can solve much more of the dataset than previously thought
